Patch Set 1:

Sorry, missed your comment somehow.

No, it fixes the cleanup when test 32a fails.  It doesn't prevent it from failing - As described in LU-10059, the feature is broken (in a pretty challenging way), and the test fails in some configs (>= 8 OSTs) because of the bug in the feature.

So.... Where's the patch to disable lockless truncates then?

Patch Set 3:

I suggested removing it, Andreas said he'd really like it fixed, I took a shot, and fixing it looks like a lot of work.  Stopped there.

I'd be happy to submit a patch removing the option to turn it on, but...  Maybe discuss this next work?

Patch Set 3:

>  Maybe discuss this next work?

Fine with me. Just feels wrong to disable a test but still allow dangerous feature to work.

commit.subject: LU-10059 osc: Disable lockless truncate
commit.message:
LU-10059 osc: Disable lockless truncate

As described in LU-10059, lockless truncate has a difficult
to resolve deadlock.  Since it's not working, let's disable
it for now.

Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
